---
title: "Twinned Towns"
subtitle: "Tidy Tuesday Submission for 2026-05-12"
date: 2026-05-12
author: Ntobeko Sosibo
toc: false
toc-location: left
toc-title: "Outline"
toc-depth: 6
format:
  html:
    mainfont: "Roboto"
    page-layout: article
    grid:
      sidebar-width: 50px
      body-width: 3840px
      margin-width: 25px
      gutter-width: 4rem
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="www/icons/favicon.png">
    embed-resources: true
    self-contained: true
execute:
  freeze: auto
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

## My Goal  

As a Durbanite South African I'm curious about which city we're twinning with, 
extending that to finding out the cities that have tied themselves with the rest
of the country.  

Something I became curious about as I explored the data was whether South Africa
has a good twinning relationship with other BRICS nations. The BRIC was 
established between 2006 and 2009, with South Africa completing the acronym in 
2010.  

&nbsp;  

&nbsp;  


```{r}
#| code-fold: true
#| label: bric-city-twins
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 17
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| message: true
#| warning: false

#loading libraries
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(readr)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(geosphere)
  library(leaflet)
  library(sf)
  library(jsonlite)
  library(htmltools)
  library(htmlwidgets)
  library(webshot2) # for exporting the presentation panel as a high resolution image
  })

# bringing in the data data
links_with_coords <- read.csv("data/links_with_coords.csv")

# ensuring the CRS is explicitly what Leaflet wants
world_sf <- st_read("www/countries.geo.json") |> 
  st_transform(4326)

# defining the separate groups for the color palettes
south_african_links_with_coords <- links_with_coords |> 
  filter(
    links_with_coords$source_country_name == "South Africa")

south_african_links_data <- south_african_links_with_coords |>
   mutate(
     dist = distHaversine(matrix(c(source_lng, source_lat), ncol = 2), 
                          matrix(c(target_lng, target_lat), ncol = 2)) / 1000,
                          )

 # counting connections per country
 country_counts <- south_african_links_data |>
   count(target_country_name, name = "twin_count") |>
   mutate(is_brics = target_country_name %in% c("Brazil", "Russia", "India", "China", "South Africa"))
 
# combining back to the main data for plotting
 plot_data <- south_african_links_data |>
   left_join(country_counts, by = "target_country_name")

# colorNumeric creates a range based on the twin_count
orange_pal <- colorNumeric(palette = c("#f59a78", "#e65823"), domain = country_counts$twin_count)
blue_pal   <- colorNumeric(palette = c("#79cfdc", "#2C89A0"), domain = country_counts$twin_count)
 south_africa_colour <- "#e01f17"

 # define the constant source (Durban/South Africa center)
 # using coordinates approximately near Durban/central SA for the 'hub' look
 sa_source_lng <- 31.0292
 sa_source_lat <- -29.8587
 
 # joining country counts to the local GeoJSON file
 world_plot_sf <- world_sf %>%
   left_join(country_counts, by = c("name" = "target_country_name"))
  
 # preparing the spatial data with final colors
 world_plot_sf <- world_plot_sf |>
   mutate(
     is_brics = name %in% c("Brazil", "Russia", "India", "China"),
     is_south_africa = name %in% "South Africa",
     final_color = case_when(
       is_south_africa ~ "#e01f17",
       is.na(twin_count) ~ "#bababa77",              # warm gray for others
       is_brics          ~ orange_pal(twin_count),   # orange for BRICS
       TRUE              ~ blue_pal(twin_count)      # blue for non-BRICS
     )
   )

# define the bounding/framing box
# format: [lng1, lat1, lng2, lat2]
world_bounds <- list(
  c(15, -55),  # Southwest: Bottom of South America/Australia
  c(50, 85)    # Northeast: Top of Greenland/Russia
  )


# creating a dataframe for annotations
# and where the leader line points to (the country center).
labels_data <- data.frame(
  name = c(
    "China", "Brazil", "United States of America", "Germany", "France",
    "United Kingdom", "Netherlands", "Taiwan", "Mexico", "Venezuela", "Argentina",
    "Ghana", "Cameroon", "Gabon", "Algeria", "Zimbabwe", "Kenya", "Palestine", 
    "Azerbaijan", "Iran", "Portugal", "Italy", "Belgium", "Russia", "India", "South Korea"
    ),
  label_text = c("<strong>China</strong>: 4 Cities<br/>Daqing, Guangzhou<br/>Hangzhou, Nanjing",                  "<strong>Brazil</strong>: 3 Cities<br/>Campinas, Curitiba<br/>Rio de Janeiro", 
                 "<strong>United States of America</strong>: 6 Cities<br/>Chicago, Jacksonville<br/>New Orleans, Palm Desert<br/>San Francisco, Washington D.C.",
                 "<strong>Germany</strong>: 2 Cities<br/>Bremen, Aachen",
                 "<strong>France</strong>: 2 Cities<br/>Nantes, Nice",
                 "<strong>United Kingdom</strong>: 2 Cities<br/>London, Newcastle upon Tyne",
                 "<strong>Netherlands</strong>: 1 City<br/>Delft",
                 "<strong>Taiwan</strong>: 3 Cities<br/>Keelung, Tainan<br/>Taipei",
                 "<strong>Mexico</strong>: 1 City<br/>Monterrey",
                 "<strong>Venezuela</strong>: 1 City<br/>Maracaibo",
                 "<strong>Argentina</strong>: 1 City<br/>Buenos Aires",
                 "<strong>Ghana</strong>: 1 City<br/>Kumasi",
                 "<strong>Cameroon</strong>: 1 City<br/>Douala",
                 "<strong>Gabon</strong>: 1 City<br/>Libreville",
                 "<strong>Algeria</strong>: 1 City<br/>Oran",
                 "<strong>Zimbabwe</strong>: 1 City<br/>Bulawayo", 
                 "<strong>Kenya</strong>: 1 City<br/>Mombasa", 
                 "<strong>Palestine</strong>: 1 City<br/>Ramallah",
                 "<strong>Azerbaijan</strong>: 1 City<br/>Baku",
                 "<strong>Iran</strong>: 1 City<br/>Tehran",
                 "<strong>Portugal</strong>: 1 City<br/>Maia", 
                 "<strong>Italy</strong>: 1 City<br/>Verona", 
                 "<strong>Belgium</strong>: 1 City<br/>Antwerp",
                 "<strong>Russia</strong>: 1 City<br/>Saint Petersburg",
                 "<strong>India</strong>: 1 City<br/>Bhubaneswar",
                 "<strong>South Korea</strong>: 1 City<br/>Daejeon"
                 ),
  # where the text box sits
  box_lng = c(
    220,   # China
    -110,  # Brazil
    -190,  # USA
    52,    # Germany
    -32.5, # France
    -30,   # United Kingdom
    -5,    # Netherlands
    160,   # Taiwan
    -140,  # Mexico
    -130,  # Venezuela
    -120,  # Argentina
    -32,   # Ghana
    -15,   # Cameroon
    -5,    # Gabon
    -35,   # Algeria
    60,    # Zimbabwe
    70,    # Kenya
    52,    # Palestine
    65,    # Azerbaijan
    73,    # Iran
    -38,   # Portugal
    32,    # Italy
    38,    # Belgium
    130,   # Russia
    90,    # India
    165    # South Korea
    ), 
  box_lat = c(
    25,     # China
    -21.5,  # Brazil
    45,     # USA
    65,     # Germany
    55,     # France
    65,     # United Kingdom
    75,     # Netherlands
    15,     # Taiwan
    23,     # Mexico
    1,      # Venezuela
    -40,    # Argentina
    9,      # Ghana
    -15,    # Cameroon
    -40,    # Gabon
    27,     # Algeria
    -40,    # Zimbabwe
    -23,    # Kenya
    17,     # Palestine
    50,     # Azerbaijan
    36,     # Iran
    42,     # Portugal
    39,     # Italy,
    75,     # Belgium
    77,     # Russia
    -5,     # India
    42      # South Korea
    ),
  # where the circle/pointer sits (country centroids)
  point_lng = c(
    104,      # China
    -51,      # Brazil
    -100,     # USA
    10.45,    # Germany
    2.21,     # France
    -2.00,    # United Kingdom
    5.29,     # Netherlands
    120.96,   # Taiwan
    -102.55,  # Mexico
    -66.58,   # Venezuela
    -63.61,   # Argentina
    -1.02,    # Ghana
    12.35,    # Cameroon
    11.60,    # Gabon
    3.05,     # Algeria
    29.15,    # Zimbabwe
    37.90,    # Kenya
    35.23,    # Palestine
    47.57,    # Azerbaijan
    53.68,    # Iran
    -8.22,    # Portugal
    12.56,    # Italy
    4.46,     # Belgium
    105.31,   # Russia
    78.96,    # India
    127.77    # South Korea
    ),
  point_lat = c(
    35,     # China
    -14,    # Brazil
    39,     # USA
    51.16,  # Germany
    46.22,  # France
    54.00,  # United Kingdom
    52.13,  # Netherlands
    23.69,  # Taiwan
    23.63,  # Mexico
    6.42,   # Venezuela
    -38.41, # Argentina
    7.94,   # Ghana
    5.8,    # Cameroon
    -0.80,  # Gabon
    28.03,  # Algeria
    -19.01, # Zimbabwe
    -0.02,  # Kenya
    31.95,  # Palestine
    40.14,  # Azerbaijan
    32.42,  # Iran
    39.39,  # Portugal
    41.87,  # Italy
    50.50,  # Belgium
    61.52,  # Russia
    20.59,  # India
    35.90   # South Korea
    )
  )

# rendering with a CSS background override with control of ocean colour
twin_final <- leaflet(
  data = world_plot_sf,
  width = "100%",
  height = 700,
  options = leafletOptions(
    zoomControl = FALSE,
    zoomSnap = 0.1,
    zoomDelta = 0.5,
    minZoom = 2.3,
    maxBounds = world_bounds,
    maxBoundsViscosity = 0.1,
    worldCopyJump = TRUE
    )
  ) |> 
  setView(lng = 10, lat = 20, zoom = 1.7) |> 
  addPolygons(
    fillColor = ~final_color,
    color = "#FFFFFF",
    weight = 0.5,
    fillOpacity = 1
    ) 

# individual Leader Lines
# loop through the data to draw separate lines for each annotation
for(i in 1:nrow(labels_data)) {
  twin_final <- twin_final |>
    addPolylines(
      lng = c(labels_data$box_lng[i], labels_data$point_lng[i]),
      lat = c(labels_data$box_lat[i], labels_data$point_lat[i]),
      color = "#fff", 
      weight = 1.4, 
      opacity = 1
      )
  }
  
# circle dot at the country
twin_final <- twin_final |>
  addCircleMarkers(
    data = labels_data, 
    lng = ~point_lng, lat = ~point_lat,
    radius = 2, 
    color = "#fff", 
    fillOpacity = 1, 
    stroke = FALSE
  ) |>
  
  # label box
  addLabelOnlyMarkers(
    data = labels_data, 
    lng = ~box_lng, lat = ~box_lat,
    label = ~lapply(label_text, htmltools::HTML),
    labelOptions = labelOptions(
      noHide = TRUE, 
      direction = "center", 
      style = list(
        "font-family" = "Plus Jakarta Sans",
        "background-color" = "rgba(255,255,255,1)", 
        "border-radius" = "8px",
        "padding" = "6px",
        "font-size" = "12px",
        "box-shadow" = "2px 2px 5px rgba(0,0,0,0)" # ensuring no shadow
        )
      )
    ) |>
  onRender("
    function(el, x) {
      el.style.backgroundColor = '#c3c3c355';
    }
  ")
 

# presentation base container (this is the canvass)
twin_final_viz <- 
  htmltools::tagList(
  tags$div(
    style = "
      background-color: white;
      padding: 50px;
      margin-left: 20px;
      margin-right: 50px;
      margin-bottom: 20px;
      ",
    
  # responsive flexbox for the brics logo and headline
 tags$div(
   style = "
    display: flex; 
    flex-wrap: wrap; 
    gap: 30px; 
    align-items: flex-start; 
    width: 100%;
  ", 
   
   # icon wrapper - width now matches the image inside
   tags$div(
     style = "
      flex: 0 0 150px;
      display: flex; 
      flex-direction: column;
      margin-bottom: 25px;
      margin-right: 25px;
    ",
     # brics logo
     tags$img(
       src = "images/brics_logo.png",     
       width = "180px",             
       height = "auto",
       style = "margin-bottom: 15px;"
     ) 
   ), 
   
   # headline and subtitle wrapper
   tags$div(
     style = "
      flex: 1;
      min-width: 400px; 
      display: flex; 
      flex-direction: column;
      padding-bottom: 20px;
    ",
     # title
     tags$div(
       style = "text-align:left; margin-bottom: 15px; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans'; font-weight: 950; line-height: 1.1;",
       HTML(
         "<span style='color:#e01f17; font-size:44px;'> SOUTH AFRICA'S </span> <span style='color:#334; font-size:44px;'> RELATIONSHIP WITH ITS </span> <span style='color:#e65823; font-size:44px;'> BRICS </span> <span style='color:#334; font-size:44px;'> AND </span> <span style='color:#2C89A0; font-size:44px;'> NON-BRICS </span> <span style='color:#334; font-size:44px;'> CITY SISTERHOOD </span>"
       )
     ),
     # subtitle
     tags$div(
       style = "text-align:left; font-family: 'Roboto'; font-size:26px; font-weight: 300; color:#888; line-height:1.4; margin-bottom:30px;",
       HTML(
         "As the last country to join what would become known as the core <b>BRICS</b> block, South Africa has an uneven balance of sister city ties with <b> Russia</b> and <b>India</b> - the latter being more notable because of the many connections in culture and industry. Given the large Indian diaspora in Durban, it can understood that many of the ties that exist are more informally categorised relationships."
       )
     )
   )
 ),

# responsive flexbox that holds the chart
tags$div(
  style = "
    display: flex; 
    flex-wrap: wrap; 
    gap: 20px; 
    align-items: flex-start; 
    width: 100%;
  ",
  
  # world map wrapper
  tags$div(
    style = "
      flex: auto; 
      height: 700px;
      min-width: 500px; 
      display: flex; 
      flex-direction: column;
    ",
    
    # world map
    twin_final
  )
), # closing responsive flexbox that holds both charts


# start of page footer with data and author details
tags$div(
      style = "text-align: center; margin-top: 50px; margin-bottom: 10px;",
    

  tags$span(
    "Data Source :",
    style = "color: #888; text-decoration: none; font-family: Plus Jakarta Sans; font-weight: 450;"),

  # tidy tuesday repo link
  tags$a(
    "Twin Towns, Twin Cities Explorer (Wikidata Query Service) via Tidy Tuesday (2026-19)",
    href = "https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/main/data/2026/2026-05-12/readme.md",
    target = "_blank",
    style = "color: #e01f17; text-decoration: none; font-family: Plus Jakarta Sans; font-weight: bold;"
    ),

  HTML("&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;"),

  # linkedin logo
  tags$img(
    HTML('<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="20" height="20" fill="#e01f17" class="bi bi-linkedin" viewBox="0 0 16 16">
  <path d="M0 1.146C0 .513.526 0 1.175 0h13.65C15.474 0 16 .513 16 1.146v13.708c0 .633-.526 1.146-1.175 1.146H1.175C.526 16 0 15.487 0 14.854zm4.943 12.248V6.169H2.542v7.225zm-1.2-8.212c.837 0 1.358-.554 1.358-1.248-.015-.709-.52-1.248-1.342-1.248S2.4 3.226 2.4 3.934c0 .694.521 1.248 1.327 1.248zm4.908 8.212V9.359c0-.216.016-.432.08-.586.173-.431.568-.878 1.232-.878.869 0 1.216.662 1.216 1.634v3.865h2.401V9.25c0-2.22-1.184-3.252-2.764-3.252-1.274 0-1.845.7-2.165 1.193v.025h-.016l.016-.025V6.169h-2.4c.03.678 0 7.225 0 7.225z"/>
</svg>')
    ), 

  # linkedin link
  tags$a(
    "  Ntobeko Sosibo  ",
    href = "https://www.linkedin.com/in/ntobeko-sosibo/",
    target = "_blank",
    style = "margin-left: 10px; color: #888; text-decoration: none; font-family: Plus Jakarta Sans; font-weight: 450;"
    ),
  
  HTML("&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;"),

  # github logo
  tags$img(
    HTML(
      '<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="20" height="20" fill="#e01f17" class="bi bi-github" viewBox="0 0 16 16">
  <path d="M8 0C3.58 0 0 3.58 0 8c0 3.54 2.29 6.53 5.47 7.59.4.07.55-.17.55-.38 0-.19-.01-.82-.01-1.49-2.01.37-2.53-.49-2.69-.94-.09-.23-.48-.94-.82-1.13-.28-.15-.68-.52-.01-.53.63-.01 1.08.58 1.23.82.72 1.21 1.87.87 2.33.66.07-.52.28-.87.51-1.07-1.78-.2-3.64-.89-3.64-3.95 0-.87.31-1.59.82-2.15-.08-.2-.36-1.02.08-2.12 0 0 .67-.21 2.2.82.64-.18 1.32-.27 2-.27s1.36.09 2 .27c1.53-1.04 2.2-.82 2.2-.82.44 1.1.16 1.92.08 2.12.51.56.82 1.27.82 2.15 0 3.07-1.87 3.75-3.65 3.95.29.25.54.73.54 1.48 0 1.07-.01 1.93-.01 2.2 0 .21.15.46.55.38A8.01 8.01 0 0 0 16 8c0-4.42-3.58-8-8-8"/>
</svg>'
    )
  ),
  
  # github link
  tags$a(
    "afrikaniz3d-za",
    href = "https://github.com/afrikaniz3d-za",
    target = "_blank",
    style = "margin-left: 10px; color: #888; text-decoration: none; font-family: Plus Jakarta Sans; font-weight: 450;"
    ),
  
  HTML("&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;"), 

  # bluesky logo
  tags$img(
    HTML(
      '<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="20" height="20" fill="#e01f17" class="bi bi-bluesky" viewBox="0 0 16 16">
  <path d="M3.468 1.948C5.303 3.325 7.276 6.118 8 7.616c.725-1.498 2.698-4.29 4.532-5.668C13.855.955 16 .186 16 2.632c0 .489-.28 4.105-.444 4.692-.572 2.04-2.653 2.561-4.504 2.246 3.236.551 4.06 2.375 2.281 4.2-3.376 3.464-4.852-.87-5.23-1.98-.07-.204-.103-.3-.103-.218 0-.081-.033.014-.102.218-.379 1.11-1.855 5.444-5.231 1.98-1.778-1.825-.955-3.65 2.28-4.2-1.85.315-3.932-.205-4.503-2.246C.28 6.737 0 3.12 0 2.632 0 .186 2.145.955 3.468 1.948"/>
</svg>'
    )
    ),

  # bluesky link
  tags$a(
    "afrikaniz3d",
    href = "https://bsky.app/profile/afrikaniz3d.bsky.social",
    target = "_blank",
    style = "margin-left: 10px; color: #888; text-decoration: none; font-family: Plus Jakarta Sans; font-weight: 450;"
    )
  ) # closing page footer with data and author details
)
)

twin_final_viz

#------------------------------------------------------
# exporting the presentation as a high resolution image
#------------------------------------------------------
  
# writing the html file directly to the project folder
 htmltools::save_html(twin_final_viz, "temp_twin_final_render.html")


# webshot "takes" a picture of the html file, saving it in the same directory
webshot2::webshot(
  url = "temp_twin_final_render.html",
  file = "Twin_Final.png",
  vwidth = 1920,   
  vheight = 1080,
  delay = 3,       # extra time for any animations to complete playing
  zoom = 2         # double pixel density for sharpness
  )
```


&nbsp;

&nbsp;
